reforms.description: The government plans to introduce multi-annual expenditure ceilings to improve fiscal sustainability and enhance compliance with budgetary planning processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving fiscal sustainability, the reform can help maintain economic stability and support long-term growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans several reforms to enhance the fiscal framework, including the introduction of multi-annual expenditure ceilings.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of expenditure ceilings is a significant reform aimed at improving fiscal discipline and sustainability, which is crucial for long-term economic health.
reforms.source_quote: The planned introduction of spending limits is welcome as expenditure rules tend to improve fiscal sustainability.

reforms.description: Slovakia plans to expand the use of direct R&D support, such as grants, and make the R&D tax allowance refundable for small and young firms to enhance business R&D and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases financial resources available for R&D, encouraging innovation and productivity growth in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan includes measures to enhance business R&D support.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to R&D funding, aiming to stimulate innovation in the private sector, which is crucial for economic recovery and growth.
reforms.source_quote: Business R&D and the share of innovative companies is very low. The plan includes expanding the use of direct R&D support, such as grants.

reforms.description: The Slovak government aims to reduce the administrative burden on start-ups by introducing 'silence is consent' licensing rules, facilitating easier business operations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying licensing processes encourages entrepreneurship and innovation by reducing barriers to entry for new businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan includes measures to reduce the administrative burden on start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses a key barrier for start-ups, potentially leading to increased innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The administrative burden on start-ups is relatively high. Reduce the administrative burden on start-ups by introducing 'silence is consent' licensing rules.

reforms.description: The government has set up an ambitious recovery programme that emphasizes reforms and investment in innovation to enhance the country's capacity to innovate and adopt new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in innovation is expected to enhance productivity and economic convergence, which can lead to higher living standards.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set up an ambitious recovery programme emphasizing reforms and investment in innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards prioritizing innovation as a key driver for economic recovery and growth, which is crucial for addressing long-term challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set up an ambitious recovery programme, emphasising reforms and investment in education, health care, a greener economy and innovation.

reforms.description: The Recovery and Resilience Plan includes a significant investment of EUR 0.7 billion aimed at improving the coordination of innovation policy, supporting research collaboration between companies, academia, and research institutes, and enhancing R&D investment in decarbonisation and digitalisation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D investment and collaboration is expected to drive innovation and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan is actively being executed with allocated funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how innovation policy is coordinated and funded, with significant implications for the economy's transition towards decarbonisation and digitalisation.
reforms.source_quote: The plan contains 66 structural reforms as well as planned investment spending of EUR 6.575 billion... in the area of Science, research and innovation.

reforms.description: The Slovak government launched the 2030 Digital Transformation Strategy to enhance the digital economy and improve the adoption of digital technologies across sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to foster technology diffusion, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government adopted the strategy and action plan for improving Slovakia's position in the digital economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing Slovakia's digital capabilities, which is crucial for future economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched the 2030 Digital Transformation Strategy for Slovakia.

reforms.description: The government adopted a Strategy and Action Plan for Improving Slovakia’s position in the Digital Economy and Society Index (DESI) until 2025, which includes measures to enhance digital skills and infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital skills and infrastructure, the strategy aims to enhance productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government adopted the strategy and action plan for improving Slovakia's position in the DESI.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses critical gaps in digital skills and infrastructure, which are essential for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2021 the government adopted a Strategy and Action Plan for Improving Slovakia’s position in the Digital Economy and Society Index (DESI) until 2025.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to increase R&D funding from the national budget by 0.05% per year over the period 2021-30 to reduce dependence on EU structural funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and stimulate economic growth by fostering research activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to increase R&D funding from the national budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhance national R&D funding, addressing a critical gap in Slovakia's innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: To reduce dependence on EU funds, it will be important that the government follows through with its commitment to increase R&D funding from the national budget by 0.05% per year over the period 2021-30.

reforms.description: The government plans to introduce pilot individual learning accounts from 2021 to 2027 to enhance participation in lifelong learning, similar to successful schemes in other countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing access to lifelong learning, the reform aims to improve the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to introduce pilot individual learning accounts from 2021 to 2027.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant step towards improving lifelong learning opportunities, which is crucial for enhancing the skills of the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: Slovakia also plans to introduce pilot individual learning accounts from 2021 to 2027, similar to schemes in Scotland, Canada, France and the Netherlands.

reforms.description: The Slovak government introduced a new R&D tax allowance in 2015, which was significantly increased in 2018 and 2020 to 200% of eligible R&D expenditure, making it one of the most generous in the OECD. However, there are plans to reduce this allowance to 100% in 2022, which may impact the support for innovation among firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The generous tax allowance is designed to lower the costs of R&D for firms, thereby encouraging innovation and technology adoption.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax allowance was increased to 200% and has been utilized by firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the support for R&D, aiming to enhance the innovation capacity of firms in Slovakia.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced a new R&D tax allowance in 2015 and increased the tax allowance significantly in 2018 and 2020 to 200% of eligible R&D expenditure.

reforms.description: The Slovak government plans to establish a single entity to coordinate the research and innovation support ecosystem, addressing the current fragmentation in governance and policy implementation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining governance is expected to enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies and improve collaboration between public and private sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: As part of the recovery programme, the government plans to establish a single entity to coordinate the research and innovation support ecosystem.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to fundamentally change the governance structure of innovation policy in Slovakia, which is crucial for effective implementation.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the recovery programme the government plans to establish a single entity to coordinate the research and innovation support ecosystem.

reforms.description: Slovakia plans to adopt a national lifelong learning strategy and introduce pilot individual learning accounts to support adult learning, particularly for older workers. This initiative aims to enhance skills and employability in response to changing job requirements due to automation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the skills of older workers, the reform aims to enhance their employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to adopt the strategy and introduce the accounts.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards enhancing the skills of older workers, which is crucial for adapting to labor market changes and improving overall productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to adopt a national lifelong learning strategy and introduce pilot individual learning accounts in 2021.
